title: cracca leucantha
intro: Cracca leucantha, also known as Lupinus leucanthus, is a species of perennial flowering plant in the lupine family. It is primarily found in the western and central United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cracca leucantha include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing, with herbal preparations typically involving teas and infusions made from its leaves and flowers.
